mnemonics: NE - Nash Equilibrium: Think 'Never Enough' players wanting to switch strategies for a better payoff.
keyPoints: A Nash Equilibrium is a state where no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy.
Competitive games often model conflict where players' interests are strictly opposed.
Zero-sum games are a subset of competitive games where one player's gain is exactly another's loss.
commonMistakes: Assuming all competitive games are zero-sum; many are not.
Confusing a dominant strategy (best no matter what) with a Nash Equilibrium (best given others' choices).
